title: Consultation reminder: animal health and welfare mandatory measures

content.text: Last month, we published a post about a consultation on proposed measures to improve livestock health and welfare in England.
This is a reminder that the consultation will close on 18 May at 23:59. If you have not yet responded, we encourage you to share your views before the deadline.
We’d like to hear your views on the following proposals:
If you farm cattle, sheep or pigs, or work in the wider livestock sector, these proposals could affect how you manage animal health, disease control and work with your vet in the years ahead.
Your feedback will help us develop these measures and shape how they will work in practice.
We read all comments on blog posts, but they are not counted as part of the consultation.
To share your views, use the Defra Citizen Space consultation hub.
If you cannot use Citizen Space, download the consultation documents at the bottom of the consultation page and send your response by email to AHWP-Mandatory@defra.gov.uk or by post to:
Universal Participation Team Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs Seacole Building, 2nd Floor 2 Marsham Street London SW1P 4DF
This consultation applies to farmers in England only.
Last month, we hosted a one-hour webinar for anyone interested in responding to the Animal Health and Welfare Pathway mandatory proposals consultation.
The session explained what the consultation is asking, the types of evidence that are most helpful, and how to shape your response so it reflects your experience.
